content:
# Baseline Render Speed — dorea grade (2026-04-02)

## Test File
- **Input**: `footage/raw/2025-11-01/DJI_20251101111428_0055_D.MP4`
- **Resolution**: 3840x2160 @ 119.88fps, 14.0s duration, 1671 frames, 218MB

## Output
- **File**: `working/graded/DJI_20251101111428_0055_D_graded.mp4`
- **Codec**: H.264 (libx264 CRF 18 fast) + AAC audio passthrough
- **Size**: 202MB

## Render Speed
- **Total wall time**: 38m 0.6s
- **Calibration phase** (RAUNE-Net + Depth Anything on 20 keyframes): ~2m 53s
- **Per-frame grading** (1671 frames): ~35m 7s
- **Frame rate**: ~0.74 fps (134s per 100 frames)
- **Realtime ratio**: 0.006x (14s video / 38 min processing)
- **CPU time**: 41m 39s user, 4m 0s sys

## Pipeline Config
- **Binary**: dorea-cli @ commit 7970229 (post inference-proxy-resize PR #13)
- **Proxy size**: 518px (default) — frames downscaled before depth inference
- **RAUNE-Net**: loaded from default path (weights_95.pth), ran on CUDA during calibration
- **Depth Anything V2**: CUDA, per-frame inference at proxy resolution
- **Grading**: CUDA (LUT + HSL + depth-aware ambiance)
- **Encoder**: libx264 (NVENC not available in this container)
- **GPU**: RTX 3060 6GB VRAM

## Bottleneck
Per-frame depth inference dominates (~1.3s/frame). IPC overhead: PNG encode → base64 → JSON over stdin/stdout pipe to Python subprocess. Potential speedups: batch inference, shared-memory IPC, or ONNX runtime to skip Python entirely.

Addressed by: docs/decisions/2026-04-02-grading-pipeline-perf-v3.md (Opt 1: motion-adaptive depth interpolation, Opt 3: raw RGB IPC, Opt 4: HF processor bypass)
